data.title: CF PharmTech’s Budesonide Nasal Spray Application Accepted by China’s NMPA
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

CF PharmTech. Inc. Class H ( (HK:2652) ) has shared an update.

CF PharmTech, Inc. has had its marketing authorization application for a budesonide nasal spray accepted by China’s National Medical Products Administration, covering indications such as seasonal and perennial allergic and non-allergic rhinitis as well as prevention and treatment of nasal polyps in patients aged six and above. The move expands the company’s already diversified nasal spray portfolio and underscores its strategic push in the respiratory inhalation segment, though the company cautions that acceptance is only an initial administrative step and that regulatory approval is not guaranteed given the inherent risks and uncertainties in drug development and commercialization.

More about CF PharmTech. Inc. Class H

CF PharmTech, Inc. is a PRC-incorporated pharmaceutical company focused on respiratory and nasal drug delivery, leveraging a nasal delivery technology platform to develop complex nasal spray formulations. Its product matrix spans multiple indications and full-course management for respiratory and rhinitis conditions, including reimbursed combination antihistamine-corticosteroid sprays and first-line therapies for allergic rhinitis across various age groups.

data.title: Jiangsu Lopal Tech Forecasts Sharply Narrowed Loss for 2025 Amid Margin Pressure
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Jiangsu Lopal Tech Co. Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2465) ) has shared an announcement.

Jiangsu Lopal Tech. Group Co., Ltd. has issued an inside information announcement estimating that it will remain loss-making in 2025, but with a markedly narrower loss compared with 2024. The company projects net loss attributable to shareholders of between RMB180.17 million and RMB147.99 million, a substantial improvement from last year’s loss of about RMB645 million, and expects net loss after deducting non-recurring items to shrink to roughly RMB29.19–39.50 million from nearly RMB698.47 million previously. Management attributes the continued losses to low gross margins in its lithium iron phosphate cathode materials business amid lithium carbonate price volatility, as well as inventory and asset impairments and significant non-recurring losses related to fair value changes of financial liabilities. Nonetheless, the company notes that cost-cutting, product differentiation, overseas expansion efforts, and efficiency initiatives have significantly reduced its loss, suggesting gradual operational stabilization despite ongoing margin and market pressures.

More about Jiangsu Lopal Tech Co. Ltd. Class H

Jiangsu Lopal Tech. Group Co., Ltd. is a China-based company listed in Hong Kong that operates in the lithium materials sector, with a key focus on lithium iron phosphate cathode materials used in batteries. Its business is sensitive to raw material price fluctuations, particularly lithium carbonate, and its profitability is also influenced by inventory valuation and asset impairment trends.

Average Trading Volume: 14,491,749

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$11.88B

data.title: Dingyi Group Buys Majority Stake in Zambian Gold Mining License Holder
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest update is out from Dingyi Group Investment Limited ( (HK:0508) ).

Dingyi Group Investment Limited has agreed to acquire a 70% equity interest in a Zambian holding company that controls a mining license granting exclusive rights to develop and mine gold and other mineral deposits at a site near Lusaka, Zambia. The US$3.42 million initial cash consideration for the stake, which may be adjusted based on verified gold deposit quantities and includes profit‑sharing rights for the vendors, will be funded through a shareholder loan from Dingyi to its purchasing subsidiary. Concurrently, Dingyi is entering into a shareholders’ agreement with a manager‑owned entity, under which the manager will provide mine administration services and hold a minority interest in the purchaser, leaving the Zambian target as an indirect, non‑wholly owned subsidiary with Dingyi holding effective control. As the mining asset is at a greenfield stage with no prior exploration or operations, the transaction marks a strategic entry into early‑stage gold mining in Africa and is classified as a disclosable transaction under Hong Kong Listing Rules, signaling a potentially higher‑risk, higher‑reward diversification of Dingyi’s portfolio that could reshape its resource‑sector positioning if the mine proves commercially viable.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0508) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.62 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Dingyi Group Investment Limited stock, see the HK:0508 Stock Forecast page.

More about Dingyi Group Investment Limited

Dingyi Group Investment Limited, incorporated in Bermuda and listed in Hong Kong (Stock Code: 508), operates through investment holdings and is expanding into natural resources by acquiring interests in mining assets overseas. The company is now targeting gold and other mineral deposits in Zambia, reflecting a strategic move into African mining projects via indirect, non-wholly owned subsidiaries and partnerships with local operators and managers.

Average Trading Volume: 611,282

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$595.5M

The latest announcement is out from i-CABLE Communications ( (HK:1097) ).

i-CABLE Communications has announced a leadership reshuffle effective 29 January 2026, with Chief Executive Officer, executive director and authorised representative Mr. To Chi Hak stepping down for personal reasons, though he indicated no disagreement with the board and no issues requiring shareholder attention. Under her new service agreement, Ms. Wong will receive a fixed monthly salary and a one-off initial payment, along with performance-based discretionary bonuses, aligning her incentives with the company’s operational performance and market conditions.

China Sunshine Paper Holdings Co. Ltd. ( (HK:2002) ) has issued an announcement.

China Sunshine Paper Holdings has announced boardroom changes effective 29 January 2026, with non-executive director Zhang Xiaohui and independent non-executive director Wang Zefeng resigning due to personal engagements and relinquishing their roles on the remuneration, nomination and audit committees. Both departing directors stated they had no claims against the company or disagreements with the board, while the company simultaneously appointed industry veteran Chen Dongxu as a non-executive director and Li Hengyuan as an independent non-executive director, moves that refresh the board’s composition and committee leadership and may influence the group’s governance and strategic oversight without disrupting continuity. Mr. Chen brings over three decades of management experience, including senior roles at Shanghai-listed Xiamen C&D Inc., and will serve a three-year term without director’s service fees, underscoring a focus on experienced oversight and alignment with Hong Kong listing requirements.

C&D Newin Paper & Pulp Corporation Limited announced the passing of independent non-executive director and audit committee chairman Ernest Wong Yiu Kit, leaving the board and audit committee temporarily non-compliant with Hong Kong Listing Rules regarding the minimum number of independent directors and audit committee members. data.title: China Southern Airlines Plans Auditor Change After Decade With KPMG
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

China Southern Airlines Company Limited Class H ( (HK:1055) ) just unveiled an announcement.

China Southern Airlines has announced a planned change of auditors following a public tender process conducted under rules governing state-owned and listed companies’ appointment of accounting firms. After KPMG Huazhen LLP and KPMG serving as auditors for ten consecutive years, the board, acting on the recommendation of its Audit and Risk Management Committee, has resolved to propose ShineWing Certified Public Accountants as the company’s 2026 domestic and internal control auditor and Shinewing (HK) CPA Limited as its 2026 international auditor, subject to shareholder approval and completion of acceptance procedures. KPMG will retire upon completing the audit of the company’s 2025 annual results and the publication of the 2025 annual report, with both the company and KPMG confirming there are no disagreements over work arrangements, fees or audit opinions, signalling a routine rotation that underscores regulatory compliance and continuity in financial oversight for investors and other stakeholders.

Listed in Hong Kong, the company serves domestic and international markets and is part of China’s state-owned enterprise sector, subject to related regulatory and governance requirements.

data.title: Zylox-Tonbridge Forecasts 139% Profit Surge on Strong Domestic and Overseas Device Sales
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An announcement from Zylox-Tonbridge Medical Technology Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2190) ) is now available.

Zylox-Tonbridge Medical Technology has issued a positive profit alert, indicating that net profit attributable to shareholders for 2025 is expected to exceed RMB240 million, a year-on-year increase of about 139%, on revenues projected to surpass RMB1.05 billion, up roughly 35% from 2024. The company attributes this strong performance to robust domestic demand for its core neurovascular and vascular products, more than 100% growth in international sales driven by broader regional market penetration, and improved operational efficiency alongside stable gross margins, signalling accelerated scale-up of its business and strengthening competitive position in both China and overseas markets ahead of its final audited results due by end-March 2026.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2190) stock is a Hold with a HK$28.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Zylox-Tonbridge Medical Technology Co., Ltd. Class H stock, see the HK:2190 Stock Forecast page.

More about Zylox-Tonbridge Medical Technology Co., Ltd. Class H

Zylox-Tonbridge Medical Technology Co., Ltd. is a China-based medical device company focused on neurovascular and peripheral vascular interventions. Its flagship products include the Kylin Flow Diverter, Neurovascular Guidewire, UltraFree Drug-Coated PTA Balloon Catheter and the Swan Endovenous Radiofrequency Ablation Catheter, serving both the domestic Chinese market and an expanding international footprint.

Average Trading Volume: 722,075

data.title: Shanghai Zhida Forms Joint Venture With Chery Unit to Drive Global Smart EV Charging Expansion
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest update is out from Shanghai Zhida Technology Development Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2650) ).

Shanghai Zhida Technology Development Co., Ltd. has entered into a joint venture with Anhui Chery Green Energy Technology Co., Ltd., a subsidiary of Chery Holding Group, to deepen its presence in the global new energy vehicle smart charging market. Leveraging Chery’s experience in charging station investment and operations and Shanghai Zhida’s smart charging technologies, the partners aim to build a closed-loop ecosystem from vehicle manufacturing to energy supply and to promote Chinese intelligent manufacturing standards worldwide. The new joint venture, Anhui Ruizhi New Energy Technology Co., Ltd., based in Anqing Economic Development Zone with registered capital of RMB20 million, will manufacture and sell EV chargers and automated charging robots, provide energy storage technical services, and handle technology import/export and international freight forwarding. Strategically, the venture will integrate charging station technologies with automated charging robotics, establish overseas R&D centres, deploy localized charging infrastructure along Chery’s international sales routes in Europe, the Middle East, Latin America and Southeast Asia, and evolve from equipment supply to comprehensive mobility and distributed energy management services, enabling Shanghai Zhida to rapidly expand capacity and market reach while mitigating capital-intensive risks and accelerating its global ecological deployment.

More about Shanghai Zhida Technology Development Co., Ltd. Class H

Shanghai Zhida Technology Development Co., Ltd. is a global smart charging solutions provider with advanced technology, comprehensive manufacturing capabilities and a multi-country service network, focused on the research, development, production, sales and servicing of new energy vehicle charging equipment.

Average Trading Volume: 62,550
